summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 2: re-detect against the now-fixed main (15 bugs resolved in rounds 0-1). Fan-out by domain cluster, filter the seen-registry, adversarially verify + classify. Also explicitly re-check the bulk-delete-no-confirm residual. Stop condition: zero new framework bugs for 2 consecutive rounds.
logs: Round 2 detection: 7 from clusters + 1 residual re-check = 8 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 2 verify: 8 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: ExportAction bulk export produces an empty CSV — controller never passes the table's columns to the action
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:176-178 (controller never passes Table::getColumns() to the action); enabling-defect: packages/export/src/Actions/ExportAction.php:47,114,124 (columns default [] and are only fillable via withColumns(), which nothing calls)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In ResourceController::bulkAction, before dispatch, if the bulk action exposes withColumns() and the table exposes getColumns(), inject the serialized columns: `if (method_exists($bulkAction,'withColumns') && method_exists($table,'getColumns')) { $bulkAction->withColumns($serializedColumns); }` — i.e. wire Table::getColumns() into ExportAction so execute() receives real column descriptors instead of an empty array.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning:
CONFIRMED and reproduced on current main (commit e1ca4e0). I could not refute it. Empirical repro with the EXACT showcase config `ExportAction::make('export')->format(ExportFormat::CSV)` (PostResource.php:150, no withColumns()): execute($records, []) writes a 6-byte file = UTF-8 BOM (efbbbf) + empty header line + empty data rows (hex efbbbf0a0a0a, content `[BOM]


`). Zero columns, zero usable data. Code path is airtight: (1) ExportAction.php:47 `$columns = []` default, only set by withColumns() (line 80); (2) grep across core/export/table shows withColumns() is called NOWHERE in production code (only the action itself + tests); (3) ResourceController::bulkAction (line 176-178) dispatches `$bulkAction->execute($records, $data)` where $data = `$request->except([...])` (request INPUT, lines 162-166), never columns; (4) CsvExporter.export with $columns=[] -> addHeader([]) and buildRow returns [] per row (CsvExporter.php:33,89-97); (5) controller discards execute()'s return payload and flashes bulk_completed (line 180). This is framework-bug not app-misuse: the showcase uses the package's advertised public API verbatim (README:26 lists it as a working 'ExportAction (CSV) bulk action'; the class docblock claims EXPORT-005 'wires execute() to call the right Exporter and write the file'). No documented requirement obliges the app to hand-duplicate its table columns via withColumns(), and the execute(mixed,array) signature receives request input, never columns — so even a correctly-written app cannot make this work. The framework HAS the data at the seam (findBulkAction already holds `$instance->table()` and Table::getColumns() exists, Table.php:407) but never forwards it. This is distinct from round-1 fix #48, which only made execute() get *called* (previously no-opped); #48 wired no column source, so the now-running export emits an empty file. Severity high (not blocker): a headline showcased feature is silently broken end-to-end producing a useless artifact with no error, but it is not data-loss/security and the file is discarded by the controller anyway. SECONDARY TASK (bulk-delete-no-confirm-residual): CONFIRMED STILL PRESENT on main. packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelIndexPage.tsx:188-217 renders each bulk action as a <button> whose onClick (line 192) fires `router.visit(url, {method, data:{record_ids}})` IMMEDIATELY (line 206) with NO check of action.requiresConfirmation and no AlertDialog/confirm gating anywhere in the bulk path. Bulk delete still fires instantly on click without confirmation.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: ResourceController::bulkAction (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:176-178) dispatches `$bulkAction->execute($records, $data)` where `$data = $request->except([...])` (request input, NOT columns). ExportAction (packages/export/src/Actions/ExportAction.php:46-47,114-133) defaults `$columns = []` and only fills it via `withColumns()`, which nothing calls. CsvExporter (packages/export/src/Exporters/CsvExporter.php:30-42,76-82) emits `addHeader([])` + empty rows when `$columns === []`. Reproduced live with the exact showcase config: `ExportAction::make('export')->format(ExportFormat::CSV)->execute($posts, [])` writes a file whose entire content is a BOM with zero headers and zero data (observed: file content = `\xEF\xBB\xBF\n\n`, FILE EXISTS yes but empty). PostResource docblock advertises this as 'a CSV export bulk action. Primary probe target'. This is NOT the round-1 fix #48: #48 only made execute() get *called* (it was previously gated on hasCallback and no-opped); it did not wire any column source, so the now-running export produces an empty file. Nothing in core or export derives ExportAction columns from `Table::getColumns()`. Expected: clicking Export yields a CSV with the table's columns and selected rows; observed: an empty BOM-only CSV silently written to sys_get_temp_dir() (then discarded — controller ignores the return payload and flashes bulk_completed).

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: WidgetDataController::show returns ['data'=>...] vs WidgetRenderer mergeData expecting unwrapped object
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Deferred/polling widget data endpoint returns {data: {...}} but WidgetRenderer consumes it unwrapped -> cards render blank after lazy-fetch/poll
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In WidgetRenderer.doFetch, unwrap the controller envelope before storing: const body = await fetcher(url); const result = (body && typeof body === 'object' && 'data' in body) ? (body as {data: unknown}).data : body; setData(result). (Equivalently, change WidgetDataController to return response()->json($widget->data()) bare — but unwrapping client-side is the minimal/defensive fix and matches the docblock promise.) Also add a regression test asserting the rendered value using the REAL {data:{...}} shape, and fix the polling test to mock {data:{value:20}} + assert the rendered number.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely on current main (branch main, e1ca4e0). Wrote a throwaway test feeding WidgetRenderer the EXACT controller shape {data:{value:99,color:'success'}} via the fetcher: StatCard rendered the null fallback '—' instead of 99 (test FAILED). A parallel test with the SSR/Inertia seed shape (widget.data = unwrapped {value:42} as emitted by Widget::toArray() line 245 data=>$this->data()) rendered 42 correctly (PASSED). This proves the two paths have divergent shapes, directly contradicting WidgetRenderer.tsx docblock lines 13-15 ('payload shape stays identical to the SSR / Inertia path'). Mechanism confirmed by reading source: StatWidget::data() (StatWidget.php:167-178) returns value/color at the top level of the data object; WidgetDataController wraps it under 'data' (line 59-61); WidgetRenderer.doFetch does setData(result) with the full body (line 63), then mergeData spreads the body onto the widget (lines 104-109), so for the fetch path value/color end up nested under widget.data and StatCard's top-level widget.value reads undefined -> blank card. The SSR path works only because toArray() stores the data object directly and the renderer seeds state from widget.data (already unwrapped) on mount. This is purely framework-internal (controller + renderer contract), not showcase misuse -> framework-bug. The existing test (WidgetRenderer.test.tsx:88-105) mocks the WRONG (unwrapped) shape {value:99} and asserts only fetcher call counts, never the rendered value, so it green-lights the broken contract and masks the bug. Severity high: breaks 100% of deferred (RF-W-05) and polling (RF-W-04) widget data refresh - the cards blank/stale out after every fetch - but it is not a security or data-loss blocker, and SSR-rendered (non-deferred, non-polling) widgets are unaffected. SECONDARY note (requested side-check, bulk-delete-no-confirm-residual): on current main, ArqelIndexPage.tsx bulk actions (lines 188-217) render a raw <button> whose onClick fires router.visit(url) IMMEDIATELY; it does NOT check action.requiresConfirmation and opens NO ConfirmDialog, despite the bulk ActionSchema carrying requiresConfirmation/confirmation (types/src/actions.ts:69-71). Row actions by contrast DO honor it via ActionButton.tsx:65 -> ConfirmDialog. So the bulk-action no-confirm residual is REAL and still present on main (separate defect from the widgets bug).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Server: packages/widgets/src/Http/Controllers/WidgetDataController.php:59-61 returns response()->json(['data' => $widget->data()]) i.e. the widget payload is WRAPPED under a top-level 'data' key. Client: packages-js/ui/src/widgets/WidgetRenderer.tsx doFetch() does `setData(result)` (line 63) with the full JSON body, then mergeData() (line 104-109) spreads `result` onto the widget. For a real fetch result {data:{value:99,color:'success'}} this yields widget.data = {value,...} nested, NOT top-level value/color. StatCard (StatCard.tsx) reads widget.value/widget.color at top level, so they are undefined and the card renders empty. The SSR/Inertia path works only because Dashboard::resolve() stores widget.toArray()['data'] = the data object directly (Widget.php:245), and on mount the renderer seeds state from widget.data (the unwrapped object). So SSR shape != fetch shape, contradicting WidgetRenderer.tsx docblock line 14-15 ('payload shape stays identical to the SSR / Inertia path'). The existing test packages-js/ui/tests/widgets/WidgetRenderer.test.tsx:89 mocks fetcher.mockResolvedValue({ value: 99 }) -- the UNWRAPPED shape -- and only asserts fetcher call counts, never the rendered value, so it green-lights the wrong contract and masks the bug.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: ResourceController::bulkAction hardcodes 'id' as the primary key instead of using getKeyName()
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In bulkAction(), compute `$keyName = (new $modelClass)->getKeyName();` once and replace both `whereIn('id', $recordIds)` calls (line 160 get, line 175 delete) with `whereIn($keyName, $recordIds)` — matching the existing correct pattern in ActionController::invokeBulk (packages/actions/src/Http/Controllers/ActionController.php:94).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning:
CONFIRMED on current main (commit e1ca4e0). Verified packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php::bulkAction: line 160 `$modelClass::query()->whereIn('id', $recordIds)->get()` and line 175 `$modelClass::query()->whereIn('id', $recordIds)->delete()` both hardcode the literal column 'id'. The sibling ActionController::invokeBulk (ActionController.php:94-96) does this correctly via `(new $modelClass)->getKeyName()`, proving the framework's own convention and that ResourceController is the inconsistent outlier. For any Resource model with a non-default primary key (`protected $primaryKey = 'uuid'` and `$incrementing = false`), the selected record_ids are the model keys (uuids) but the query filters on column 'id': bulk delete silently affects zero rows (success flash, nothing deleted) and bulk export returns an empty collection — or throws SQLSTATE 42S22 "Unknown column 'id'" if no `id` column exists. This is a real correctness bug independent of validation/auth.

Classified framework-bug (not app-misuse): the API contract is that Resources may declare any model with any primary key; ResourceController is responsible for resolving by the model's real key (it already does for single-record paths — `findOrFail` uses Eloquent `find($id)` which respects getKeyName). The bulk paths are the only place it leaks a hardcoded 'id'. No app code can work around this.

Severity medium (not high/blocker): LATENT in apps/showcase — all 8 showcase models (Author, Category, Comment, Post, Setting, Tenant, Ticket, User) use the default integer 'id' primary key (grep for `primaryKey` in apps/showcase/app/Models returned nothing), so it does not manifest in the baseline app today. It only triggers for downstream apps using custom PKs, and a silent no-op delete (rather than wrong-record deletion) is data-safe, not data-destructive. Hence medium.

SECONDARY FINDING (bulk-delete-no-confirm-residual) — also CONFIRMED as a real, separate gap on main: packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelIndexPage.tsx:188-217 renders each bulk action as a plain <button> whose onClick fires `router.visit(url, { method, data:{record_ids} })` IMMEDIATELY (line 206), with NO confirm dialog and NO reference to `requiresConfirmation`/`confirmation`. The framework HAS the mechanism — ActionButton.tsx wraps single/row actions in ConfirmDialog when `action.requiresConfirmation` is set (covered by packages-js/ui/tests/ActionButton.test.tsx and present in dist/chunk-O5UY3YPT.js) — but the bulk-action rendering path does not honor it. So a destructive bulk delete fires on a single click with no confirmation. This is a distinct framework bug worth its own ticket; the primary verdict above remains the getKeyName issue.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:160 `$modelClass::query()->whereIn('id', $recordIds)->get()` and line 175 `$modelClass::query()->whereIn('id', $recordIds)->delete()` both hardcode the literal column 'id'. For any model with a non-default primary key (e.g. `protected $primaryKey = 'uuid'`), bulk delete and bulk export silently match zero records (or throw on a missing 'id' column). The sibling ActionController already does this correctly: packages/actions/src/Http/Controllers/ActionController.php:95 uses `(new $modelClass)->getKeyName()`. The showcase models all use 'id' so this is latent there, but it is a framework correctness bug. Expected: bulk actions resolve records by the model's real key column via getKeyName(); observed: 'id' is assumed.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: AiSelectField::classify() lowercases the AI response but compares against original-case option keys, so any non-lowercase option key never matches (always falls back / returns null)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In classify(), do a case-insensitive membership check: build a lowercased key map (e.g. $byLower = array_combine(array_map('mb_strtolower', array_keys($this->options)), array_keys($this->options))) and return $byLower[$candidate] ?? $this->fallbackOption; OR stop lowercasing in normalize() and instead match candidate against keys case-insensitively. Returning the original-case key preserves the option contract.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed and reproduced on current code. normalize() (AiSelectField.php:235) applies mb_strtolower() to the AI response, but classify() (line 218) checks array_key_exists($candidate, $this->options) against the ORIGINAL-case option keys. The comparison is asymmetric, so any option set with an uppercase character in a key can never match — even when the model answers with the exact key. I wrote a Pest repro against the package's own ConfigurableFakeProvider: options ['Open'=>'Open','InProgress'=>'In Progress','Resolved'=>'Resolved'], fake provider returns the exact key 'Open' -> classify() returns NULL (asserted: "Failed asserting that null is identical to 'Open'"); returns 'InProgress' -> NULL. With all-lowercase keys the field works. The package tests mask this because every fixture uses all-lowercase keys ('tech','finance','open'). This is a framework bug, not app-misuse: apps/showcase does not use AiSelectField at all (it uses the standard Field::select for STATUS_OPTIONS), and the field's docblock contract (lines 21-22, 54-57) documents no lowercase-key requirement — it tells the model to reply with the exact key, then silently destroys mixed-case keys. Severity medium rather than high: it is a real silent-failure on valid usage, but AiSelectField lives in the optional/experimental ai package, is not exercised by the baseline app, only triggers when keys contain uppercase, and has a trivial workaround. SECONDARY (bulk-delete residual, as requested): on current main, packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelIndexPage.tsx lines 188-217 render each bulk action as an inline <button> whose onClick fires router.visit(url,{method,data:{record_ids}}) IMMEDIATELY. It does NOT check action.requiresConfirmation and does NOT open any ConfirmDialog/AlertDialog — it bypasses ActionButton entirely. The action type (packages-js/types/src/actions.ts:69-71) carries requiresConfirmation/confirmation, and row actions via ActionButton.tsx:65 DO honor it, so bulk actions are an unfixed honored-contract gap: destructive bulk operations still fire with no confirmation.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/ai/src/Fields/AiSelectField.php:194-236. classify() calls normalize($result->text) which does mb_strtolower() (line 235), then checks array_key_exists($candidate, $this->options) (line 218). The option keys are never lowercased, so the comparison is asymmetric. Live repro (apps/showcase via a fixed-reply provider): options ['Open'=>'Open','InProgress'=>'In Progress','Resolved'=>'Resolved'], AI returns the EXACT key 'Open' -> classify() returns NULL; AI returns 'InProgress' -> NULL. With all-lowercase keys ['open'=>...,'in_progress'=>...] and AI returning 'open' -> classify() correctly returns 'open'. So the field silently fails for every option set whose keys contain any uppercase character, even when the model answers perfectly. The bug is masked in the package tests (packages/ai/tests/Unit/Fields/AiSelectFieldTest.php + AiClassifyControllerTest.php) because every test fixture uses all-lowercase keys ('tech','finance'). Fix: lowercase the keys for the membership check (e.g. build a lowercased key map) or stop lowercasing the candidate.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Versionable prune query keys on $model::class (FQCN) while versions are stored/queried elsewhere via getMorphClass(); apps using a morph map never prune (keep_versions silently ignored)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In pruneOldVersionsFor(), replace ->where('versionable_type', $model::class) with ->where('versionable_type', $model->getMorphClass()) so the prune predicate matches the value MorphTo::associate() actually persists.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: REPRODUCED on current main. writeVersion() persists via $version->versionable()->associate($model); Laravel's MorphTo::associate() (MorphTo.php:259) stores versionable_type = $model->getMorphClass(). versions() (morphMany) and restoreToVersion() (line 173) both key off getMorphClass(). But pruneOldVersionsFor() line 212 filters on $model::class (raw FQCN). When no morph map is set these are equal (showcase + all existing fixtures use no map: getMorphClass()===FQCN), which is why the existing prune test VersionableTraitTest:82 passes and masks the defect. I added a repro test that called Relation::enforceMorphMap(['article'=>Article::class]), set keep_versions=2, then did 4 writes. Diagnostics printed STORED versionable_type=["article"] (the alias), model::class=Arqel\\...\\Article vs getMorphClass=article, and TOTAL row count=4 with zero pruned. Asserting the alias-keyed row count == 2 failed with "4 is identical to 2" — prune silently no-ops, arqel_versions grows unbounded, keep_versions ignored. Applying the one-line getMorphClass() fix made the repro pass and the full existing suite still green (16 passed). I reverted both the repro test and the source patch; tree left clean. This is a genuine framework bug (internal inconsistency: store/query/restore use getMorphClass(), only prune uses ::class), not app misuse — apps using morph maps follow standard Laravel practice. Severity medium not low: silent unbounded table growth in production for any app calling enforceMorphMap (a very common Laravel hardening practice), data-retention/storage impact, but not a correctness/security blocker and latent for apps without a morph map. SECONDARY finding the orchestrator asked me to verify (bulk-delete confirm): on current main packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelIndexPage.tsx lines 188-217 render bulk actions as a raw button whose onClick calls router.visit(url) IMMEDIATELY with no confirm dialog and no check of action.requiresConfirmation, despite ActionSchema carrying requiresConfirmation/confirmation (types/src/actions.ts:68-71) and row actions honoring it via ActionMenu/ActionButton. So the bulk-action no-confirm residual is STILL present (not fixed) on main.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/versioning/src/Concerns/Versionable.php: writeVersion() persists via versionable()->associate($model) which stores versionable_type = $model->getMorphClass() (the morph-map ALIAS when Relation::enforceMorphMap is set). restoreToVersion() also compares against $this->getMorphClass() (line 173). But pruneOldVersionsFor() queries ->where('versionable_type', $model::class) (line 212, the raw FQCN), and getCostSince-style consistency is broken: when a morph map aliases App\Models\Post -> 'post', stored rows have versionable_type='post' but the prune WHERE looks for 'App\Models\Post' -> zero matches -> arqel-versioning.keep_versions is never enforced and the arqel_versions table grows unbounded. Latent in apps/showcase (no morph map: $model::class === getMorphClass() === 'App\Models\Post', confirmed via tinker), so it only bites apps that call Relation::enforceMorphMap. Snapshot/create/update/restore themselves verified working end-to-end. Fix: use $model->getMorphClass() in pruneOldVersionsFor().

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: WidgetRenderer filterRef write-only; data endpoint URL omits filters query param
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Selected dashboard filter values never sent to per-widget data endpoint; deferred/polling widgets ignore user filter selection
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In doFetch(), build the URL with the live filters from filterRef.current, e.g. `const qs = new URLSearchParams(); for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(filterRef.current ?? {})) qs.append(\`filters[${k}]\`, String(v)); const u = qs.toString() ? \`${url}?${qs}\` : url;` then fetch(u)/fetcher(u). filterRef is already maintained (line 44) precisely so the long-lived effect closure reads current filters without re-subscribing.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning:
CONFIRMED real framework bug; could not refute. Reproduced by reading current main (branch confirmed `main`).

Client side (packages-js/ui/src/widgets/WidgetRenderer.tsx): line 43-44 stores filterValues into filterRef and keeps it current, but `filterRef` appears exactly twice in the file (both the declaration/assignment) and `filterRef.current` is NEVER read. doFetch() (line 58-67) builds `url = /admin/dashboards/${dashboardId}/widgets/${widgetId}/data` (line 57) with no query string and no body, then calls fetcher(url) or fetch(url). No `filters` payload is ever attached. The effect deps (line 84) don't even include filterValues, so even a re-render path wouldn't help.

Server side (packages/widgets/src/Http/Controllers/WidgetDataController.php): the contract is fully implemented and waiting for the client — line 53 reads `$request->input('filters')`, line 54-57 applies `$widget->filters($filters)`. Widget.php confirms the consumer chain: `filters()` (line 124) stores them and `filterValue()` (line 143) is documented as 'the canonical reader subclasses should call inside their data() method'. So a deferred/polling widget always recomputes data() with EMPTY filters regardless of user selection.

Wiring above is live: DashboardGrid.tsx:90 passes `filterValues={values}` (the user's live DashboardFilters selection) into every WidgetRenderer, where it dies. The whole DashboardFilters -> filterValues -> server segmentation path is dead for the lazy/poll fetch path. This is framework-bug, not app-misuse: the API is wired end-to-end (DashboardGrid passes it, server reads it, Widget consumes it) and the single broken link is inside framework code (WidgetRenderer), with no way for an app to fix it without patching the package.

Severity medium (not high): no crash, no data corruption, no security/authorization issue (authz still enforced server-side). It is a silently-wrong feature. It is also LATENT in the baseline app: apps/showcase MainDashboard.php declares NO filters and uses NO deferred/poll widgets (all data resolves inline at SSR/Inertia serialisation), so the defect is not observable in showcase today — but the framework contract is genuinely broken for any dashboard that combines filters with deferred/poll widgets.

SECOND TASK (bulk-delete-no-confirm residual) — TRUTH as read on current main: STILL BROKEN. packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelIndexPage.tsx renders bulk actions as raw <button> elements (lines 188-217); the onClick (lines 192-211) fires `router.visit(url, { method, data:{record_ids}, ... })` IMMEDIATELY. It does not read action.requiresConfirmation and opens no confirm/AlertDialog (grep for confirm/Dialog/requiresConfirmation in the bulk path returns nothing). The field is real and honored elsewhere: packages-js/types/src/actions.ts:69 declares `requiresConfirmation?: true`, and packages-js/ui/src/action/ActionButton.tsx:65,100 opens a ConfirmDialog for it. But the bulk path bypasses ActionButton entirely, so a bulk delete with requiresConfirmation fires with no confirmation. This is a separate, still-open framework bug (would itself rate medium/high given destructive bulk delete without confirm).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages-js/ui/src/widgets/WidgetRenderer.tsx captures filterValues into filterRef (line 43-44: `const filterRef = useRef(filterValues); filterRef.current = filterValues;`) but filterRef.current is NEVER read anywhere in the file (grep -c filterRef.current => 1, the write at line 44). doFetch() (line 57) builds url = `/admin/dashboards/${dashboardId}/widgets/${widgetId}/data` with NO query string / filters payload and calls fetch(url) / fetcher(url). The server WidgetDataController.php:53-57 reads $request->input('filters') -- which is therefore always empty on poll/deferred refetch. Net effect: DashboardGrid passes the user's live filter selection (DashboardGrid.tsx:90 filterValues={values}) down to each WidgetRenderer, but it is dropped, so a deferred/polling widget always recomputes with empty filters instead of the user's chosen filter values. The whole DashboardFilters -> filterValues wiring is dead for the lazy-fetch path.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: ExportAction default destinationDir=sys_get_temp_dir + ResourceController::bulkAction discards execute() return + ExportDownloadController reads storage/app/arqel-exports
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Bulk ExportAction writes file to sys_get_temp_dir() and the controller discards the path; download controller reads storage/app/arqel-exports -> completed bulk export is never retrievable by the user
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Make the export round-trip coherent: (a) ExportAction::make() should default destinationDir to storage_path('app/arqel-exports') (same dir the download controller globs) instead of sys_get_temp_dir(); (b) ResourceController::bulkAction must surface the execute() return — capture $result = $bulkAction->execute(...) and flash a signed download URL (back()->with('download', route('arqel.export.download', $exportId))); and (c) align filename scheme — ExportAction writes export-<Ymd-His>.<ext> but ExportDownloadController globs export-<uuid>.*, so use a shared UUID-based id. Minimal first step: default ExportAction dir to storage_path('app/arqel-exports') + flash the produced path so the file is at least retrievable.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end against current main (HEAD e1ca4e0). All three links confirmed in code: (1) ExportAction::make() line 58 sets destinationDir = sys_get_temp_dir(); execute() (lines 120-132) writes export-<Ymd-His>.<ext> to /tmp and returns the path payload. (2) ResourceController::bulkAction line 177 calls $bulkAction->execute($records,$data) but discards the return value, then unconditionally returns back()->with('success', bulk_completed) — no path, no URL, nothing surfaced. (3) ExportDownloadController::resolveDirectory() line 76 falls back to storage_path('app/arqel-exports') because config('arqel-export.destination_dir') is NULL — verified there is NO config/ dir in the package, no mergeConfigFrom/publishes, and no showcase override. Additional kill-shot: the controller globs export-<exportId>.* where exportId must match /^[a-f0-9-]+$/ (UUID), but ExportAction names files export-<Ymd-His> (contains no valid match path and wrong dir). Showcase PostResource.php:150 wires ExportAction::make('export')->format(CSV) — the documented, supported usage; withDestinationDir is optional and wouldn't fix it anyway since the return is discarded and the filename scheme is incompatible. Therefore this is a framework defect, not app-misuse. Could NOT refute. Severity medium (not high): the ExportAction docblock lines 31-38 explicitly declares the flash-notification + signed-download-URL piece as deliberately deferred to EXPORT-006/007/008 — so this is a known-incomplete feature shipped/wired into showcase that silently misleads the user (success flash, file produced in /tmp, never retrievable), not a regression of previously-working functionality. SECOND QUESTION (bulk-delete-no-confirm residual): still present on main. ArqelIndexPage.tsx bulk button (lines 189-216) fires router.visit(url) immediately in onClick (line 206) with no confirm dialog; there is zero reference to requiresConfirmation / AlertDialog / confirm anywhere in the file. Clicking bulk-delete deletes immediately without confirmation.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: End-to-end traced. ExportAction::make() (packages/export/src/Actions/ExportAction.php:58) defaults destinationDir = sys_get_temp_dir(); execute() (line 120-132) writes file named export-<Ymd-His>.<ext> and RETURNS {path,filename,format,mimeType}. Confirmed at runtime: DEFAULT DEST = /tmp/claude-1000/export-20260606-022728.csv (file written, correct CSV content). The core bulk dispatch ResourceController::bulkAction (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:176-180) calls $bulkAction->execute($records,$data) but THROWS AWAY the return value and just returns back()->with('success', bulk_completed) -- no download URL, no flash with path, nothing surfaced to the user. Separately, ExportDownloadController (packages/export/src/Http/Controllers/ExportDownloadController.php:44-49 + resolveDirectory line 67-77) globs storage_path('app/arqel-exports')/export-<id>.* (config arqel-export.destination_dir is NULL by default, confirmed). So even if a URL were generated, the file is in /tmp, not in storage/app/arqel-exports -> 404. Showcase wires PostResource bulk action ExportAction::make('export')->format(CSV) (apps/showcase/app/Arqel/Resources/PostResource.php:150) with no withDestinationDir, so it hits exactly this dead-end. The CSV is produced but unreachable end-to-end.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Bulk delete still fires without a confirm dialog despite the action declaring requiresConfirmation
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Replace the inlined raw bulk `<button>` (which calls router.visit directly in onClick) with `<ActionButton action={action} size="sm" onInvoke={() => router.visit(action.url, { method, data: { record_ids: selectedIds }, preserveScroll: true, onSuccess: () => setSelectedIds([]) })} />` so the bulk action routes through the confirm-aware path exactly like row actions do via ActionMenu → ActionButton.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Re-verified on current main (branch main, post-13-fixes). End-to-end chain: (1) PHP Actions::deleteBulk() at packages/actions/src/Actions.php:63-70 calls ->requiresConfirmation(); (2) Action::toSchema() at Action.php:320 emits 'requiresConfirmation' => true into the serialized schema; (3) the type ActionSchema (packages-js/types/src/actions.ts:69-71) carries requiresConfirmation?: true and confirmation?: ConfirmationConfig; (4) the framework's confirm-aware primitive ActionButton (packages-js/ui/src/action/ActionButton.tsx:65,100-108) checks action.requiresConfirmation and opens ConfirmDialog; row actions correctly route through ActionMenu → ActionButton and DO confirm. However the bulk button in ArqelIndexPage.tsx:188-217 renders a raw <button> whose onClick calls router.visit(url, { method, data: { record_ids: selectedIds } }) IMMEDIATELY, never reading action.requiresConfirmation or action.confirmation. So clicking 'Delete selected' fires the destructive bulk delete with NO confirm dialog. The inline comment at lines 193-195 confirms the #48 fix only addressed the stock URL/record_ids dispatch shape — it did NOT route the bulk button through ActionButton. This is a residual framework defect (the framework's own default index page ignores the action's declared confirmation contract), not showcase misusing the API: showcase just uses the stock deleteBulk action and the framework page drops the confirmation gate. Could not refute. Severity medium: destructive bulk delete fires without the declared confirm guard (data-loss risk on a misclick), but it is gated behind explicit row selection + button click and the server still performs the delete authorized; it is a UX/safety-contract violation rather than an auth/security hole or hard crash.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Round 1 found bulk delete fires with no confirm (ArqelIndexPage.tsx:184-213 renders a raw button calling router.visit, ignoring action.requiresConfirmation/confirmation which Actions::deleteBulk() sets). The #48 fix changed the bulk URL/dispatch but may NOT have routed the bulk button through the confirm-aware ActionButton. RE-VERIFY on current main: does clicking bulk Delete-selected now open a confirm dialog? If not, this is a residual framework bug.
